hero.title: Joshua Hong
hero.metaLine: Freshman | UTSA Men's Golf | NCAA Division I
hero.definition: Dominant mid-major freshman with Power 4 translation upside.
hero.supporting: Low-variance, high-reliability scorer with proven lineup value, strong counting-round consistency, difficult-condition performance, and early evidence of both floor stability and contention ceiling.

quickSummary: Immediate lineup-value freshman with elite counting reliability and unusually low floor risk.
Proven winner with repeat top-3 and top-10 evidence rather than one isolated spike result.
Performs especially well in difficult scoring environments and has shown strong score-protection characteristics.
Projects as a strong 3–5 slot competition candidate in a stronger program, with upside beyond simple stability value.

metrics.label: Clippd Adjusted Avg
metrics.note: Advanced scoring indicator showing clear Division I lineup value.

metrics.label: Raw Scoring Avg
metrics.note: Freshman-season scoring baseline with repeat competitive stability.

metrics.label: Counting Rate
metrics.note: 26 of 27 counting rounds. High-probability travel roster value.

metrics.label: Head-to-Head
metrics.note: Strong field-beating signal beyond simple ranking position.

metrics.label: Win Rate
metrics.note: Consistent advantage over the field across the full season sample.

metrics.label: Top Finishes
metrics.note: Wins / Top-3s / Top-10s in 9 events as a freshman.

metrics.label: WAGR
metrics.note: Current global ranking; should be read alongside deeper value indicators.

metrics.label: Clippd Rank
metrics.note: Market snapshot, with underlying profile stronger than rank alone suggests.

playerType.title: Low Variance
playerType.desc: Rarely loses team value through round collapse. The scoring pattern shows strong floor protection and week-to-week dependability.

playerType.title: High Counting Reliability
playerType.desc: A 96.3% counting rate reflects real travel-roster stability and a profile coaches can trust in lineup construction.

playerType.title: Difficult-Condition Friendly
playerType.desc: Results indicate that tougher scoring environments may reveal more value, not less. He tends to protect score when fields get stretched.

playerType.title: Ceiling Plus Floor
playerType.desc: Not only a safe scorer. He has already shown win equity, podium finishes, and the ability to convert strong form into high-end outcomes.

timeline.title: Season Champion Without a Win
timeline.desc: Early proof of consistency and cumulative scoring value. The profile first emerged as stable, reliable, and difficult to beat over time.

timeline.title: Six Consecutive Wins
timeline.desc: The profile evolved from stable season-long performer into a player with real finishing power, win equity, and repeated first-place conversion.

timeline.title: Immediate NCAA Lineup Value
timeline.desc: Freshman season translated quickly into win, podium finishes, top-10 volume, and one of the clearest reliability profiles on the roster.

points: Only player under par in the field
Tough scoring environment with high separation pressure
Clear evidence of difficult-condition win equity
Evidence of top-end scoring gear, not just safe accumulation
Power-field exposure produced meaningful finishing signal
Supports translation beyond weak-field inflation concerns
Useful marker for higher-level schedule projection

underlyingIndicators: 26 of 27 rounds counted toward team score

projectedRole.body: Best read as an immediate lineup-competition player rather than a pure development-only add. The profile suggests strong travel-roster value, high counting-round probability, and a realistic pathway to 3–5 slot competition in a stronger program.
projectedRole.translationText: Reliable counting-round addition with upside beyond floor value.
projectedRole.developmentText: Stronger weekly field exposure and higher daily competition may accelerate further ranking movement.

cultureFit: Strong work ethic with consistent post-round practice habits
Comfortable in stronger fields and pressure environments
Steady competitive temperament and coachable profile
Professional conduct, team-oriented behavior, and sportsmanship value
Long-term development mindset rather than short-term comfort seeking

transferObjective.body: Seeking the next competitive step in an environment with stronger daily lineup pressure, more frequent power-field scheduling, and a long-term development structure aligned with professional golf goals.
transferObjective.noteText: This is a forward-looking competitive move, not a complaint-based narrative.
